original_hypothesis.title: emergencyWithdraw Drains All Contract ETH Without Zeroing collectionTotalAmount Accounting
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: emergencyWithdraw sends the entire MinterContract balance to adminsContract.owner() but does not zero any collectionTotalAmount entries. After withdrawal, payArtist can still be called (collectionTotalAmount > 0) but all ETH transfers will fail silently since the contract has no balance. collectionTotalAmount is then zeroed, and the artist permanently loses their funds. The withdrawn ETH goes entirely to the admin owner.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The accounting desynchronization between emergencyWithdraw (drains balance) and payArtist (reads collectionTotalAmount) means one function can drain funds that the other function is supposed to distribute. This creates a path where artist and team payments are permanently lost.

proof_of_signal.pattern: emergencyWithdraw: uint balance = address(this).balance; payable(admin).call{value: balance}. No modification of collectionTotalAmount. payArtist at line 415: royalties = collectionTotalAmount[_collectionID]; collectionTotalAmount[_collectionID] = 0; then attempts transfers from empty balance.

preconditions: Collections have non-zero collectionTotalAmount (unmade artist payments)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: emergencyWithdraw drains ALL balance including artist-owed ETH. payArtist subsequently reads stale collectionTotalAmount, zeros it, and silently fails all transfers.
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin (trusted role) uses emergencyWithdraw which has no safeguard for pending artist payments
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Admin owner receives all ETH including artist/team entitled amounts

economic_questions: Is emergencyWithdraw intended to override artist payment accounting?
What is the total ETH at risk across all collections?
Could this be used maliciously by a compromised function admin?
validation_requirements: Call emergencyWithdraw then payArtist and verify artist receives nothing
Test that payArtist's .call transfers fail silently on empty balance

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Total contract balance including all pending artist payments
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: 100% of pending payments redirected to admin

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: All artists lose pending payments; total protocol revenue at risk

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All artists with pending payments across all collections

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: emergencyWithdraw sends to adminsContract.owner(); payArtist reads collectionTotalAmount
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify accounting desync between the two functions

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
